prose:
Method–provenance block
Property: condensed Fukui function (f+ / f– / f0) and dual descriptor for each atom in acrolein; headline value reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui index f+.
Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ → B3LYP/def2-TZVP (density-fitted) in PySCF 2.x.
Geometry provenance: fixed Cartesian coordinates read from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/acrolein.xyz (no re-optimization; single-point property). This is therefore a DFT//(input) calculation.
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet for N state; doublets for N ± 1 states as required by the Fukui protocol).
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill calling PySCF (DFT) with default density fitting; partial charges via Mulliken population analysis.
Convergence & caveats: integrity.status = "ok" and integrity.trustworthy = true. The tool reports three warnings (verbatim below) indicating the usual charge-conservation checks on f± and a schema header note; sums of f+ and f– are exactly 1.0000, so no significant drift is present. No SCF or geometry-optimization failures were reported.

Selected numerical results (atomic indices are 0-based):
• Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (C, β-carbon of the C=O group) with f+ = 0.64232102.
• Most nucleophilic site: atom 0 (O) with f– = 0.39388616.
